bottom_line: The unit economics clear the bar but only just; at 8.0x ARR the deal underwrites to a sub-hurdle return unless we get price down. Fund with conditions, not at ask.
verdict_scale: GREEN fund it / YELLOW fund with cut triggers / RED kill or revise

numbers.burn_multiple_read: below the 2.0x problem line, but not efficient — every $1 of net-new ARR costs $1.80 of burn
numbers.cac_payback_read: 19 months is over the <18mo healthy line; acceptable but not a green light
numbers.rule_of_40_read: below 40 — growth 38 + FCF margin -7; the company is bought for growth, and growth is decelerating
numbers.gross_margin_read: healthy for the category and stable; cost-of-revenue grows slower than revenue
numbers.implied_multiple_vs_last_round: ask $336M < Series C $390M post -> a markdown round, seller is motivated

bear_case_survival.test: revenue at 50% of plan, does the combined thesis still clear 20% IRR?
bear_case_survival.result: FAIL at ask price; PASS if entry <= 6.5x ARR
bear_case_survival.detail: Standalone runway is only 20 months; if we don't fund the integration, the asset stalls. The downside case must be the primary input, not the bull case.

capital_allocation_alternative: Same ~$300M could fund 2 years of internal build of the RevOps capability OR a buyback; the deal must beat both on risk-adjusted IRR, and at 8.0x it does not.
conditions_if_yellow: Entry price <= 6.5x ARR ($273M) so the synergy case carries a >=20% IRR
Cut trigger: combined NRR < 105% at 12 months -> pause integration spend
Reconcile Q4 deferred-revenue schedule to GL before signing (data-room gap)
reasoning_technique: Chain of Thought — model the downside first, then the upside; never round in our favor
source: cfo-review (six CFO forcing questions: burn & runway, unit economics, capital allocation alternative, revenue quality, bear-case survival) + cfo-advisor (burn multiple, Rule of 40, LTV:CAC, CAC payback thresholds)
